title: Odd Coincidences Ive noticed in the Rebuilds
display_title: Odd Coincidences Ive noticed in the Rebuilds
wikitext:
[]: Odd Coincidences I've noticed in the Rebuilds. Post by JaerG » Tue Jan 14, 2014 7:24 pm Hey guys, I have a few things I've noticed and haven't seen talked about yet, when it comes to the original Evangelion series and the rebuilds. I would like you all to tell me what you think, or if you know something I don't tell me that also. Ok, it started with 1.11 when the water was red, I know that that was just one of the many changes they made but it made me more suspicious when Kaworu wakes up on the moon and says, "It's you AGAIN third Child." Again. As if he had been through a similar situation before. Then that lead me to believe that when Shinji rejected Human Instrumentality in the End of Evangelion things just kind of started over, thus why in the rebuild films the water is red and why Kaworu says at the end of 2.22, "This time I'll show you true happiness." I guess if this whole article has been confusing and scrambled my theory is that the recent rebuild films are actually a continuance/sequel to the original anime. Has anyone else thought of this?

[]: Post by DJGORDD » Tue Jan 14, 2014 8:23 pm I'm glad that you posted this, because after sitting down and watching all three of the released Rebuild films, I agree. I think that the first film followed the first half of the show in order to help keep everyone on the same page. Why divert too fast right away, and lose your audience. Granted, there were some things in the first film that helped to point me in this current direction. You've touched upon the water, which was the first thing that struck me. It makes sense to say that its a continuation in this sense, because at the end of EoE, the water is red after everyone else has died. Another thing that struck me about this in the first film is that no one mistook Lilith for Adam, as they did constantly in the series. Misato takes Shinji down to the LCL plant right away, and tells him about how they made a contract with Lilith. In this instance, I think that after Shinji rejected Instrumentality in EoE, once everything started over, characters retained some bits of knowledge. I didn't catch as many in the second film, aside from Kaworu making the comment about making him happy this time. In fact, a lot of what Kaworu says in the second film led me to start thinking that they were a sequel. I'd take the time to go through line by line, and if the thread lives a while I may, but at the moment I'm not going to. As for the third film, I'm not sure where it stands as far as really helping to make this point, because everything is so radically different now. But then again, the fact that everything has become so different could point to that as well. Dr. Akagi seems to have changed sides, though they never come out and say if she's been snogging Gendo from the beginning. I choose to think she was, since it would make sense for her character. I don't know if that helps, but hopefully it'll help to give us some more ideas.

[]: Re: Odd Coincidences I've noticed in the Rebuilds. Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 14, 2014 10:51 pm JaerG : "Sequel theory", as it's informally called, is by now a worn-out staple of Internet conversations involving the new movies. Most of the supposed pieces of evidence are just visual call-backs to NGE that have independent explanations in the new movies; the only things unexplained are the weird things Kaworu says. I'd suggest looking through previous threads on the topic for further elaboration: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/4369/The-Sequel-Theory-Thread/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6022/A-remake-or-a-sequel/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13416/Remake-Sequel-Discussion/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/12673/Why-do-people-hate-the-Sequel-Theory/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10973/What-does-sequel-mean-to-you/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/9474/Rebuild-not-a-sequel-but-perhaps-an-alternate-reality/ http://forum.evageeks.org/post/560857/Sequel-theory-once-again/ http://forum.evageeks.org/post/359777/My-Eva-Rebuild-Story-Theory-more-concise/ Yes, it is incredibly popular.
